acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Apply total for note/label/dep ops
- [ ] Collisions deterministic (no ApplyError)
- [ ] Dep indexes updated from OR-Set membership

**Invariants to re-verify**
- [ ] Apply never errors for missing beads on label/dep/note
- [ ] Collision resolution deterministic and convergent
- [ ] Store stamps update only on real membership change
description:
**Problem**
Apply currently errors on missing beads and collisions; deps are LWW.

**Files**
- src/core/apply.rs
- src/core/state.rs
- src/core/composite.rs
design: Implement apply handlers for LabelAdd/LabelRemove and DepAdd/DepRemove. Update note handling to store in NoteStore without MissingBead errors. Replace collision errors with deterministic resolution (note_winner + bead collision policy). Update ApplyOutcome as needed for labels/notes.
title: Apply: OR-Set ops, orphans, deterministic collisions

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] New fixture `ReplRig` can spin up N real daemons with isolated dirs under `./tmp`.
- [ ] Rig can apply ops (create/show) and assert convergence via admin watermarks/heads.
- [ ] Rig supports tailnet-style fault injection via proxies (delay/drop/dup/reorder).
- [ ] New slow integration test uses the rig and passes locally.
- [ ] Cleanup reliably shuts down daemons and proxy processes; no orphaned sockets.
- [ ] Tests run without writing outside `./tmp`.
description:
**Problem**
We have an in-process `ReplicationRig` and a single ad-hoc daemon-to-daemon test, but no reusable harness to spin up N real daemons with consistent config, fault injection, and convergence assertions. This makes it hard to add end-to-end replication tests that exercise real sockets, IPC, store discovery, and cleanup. Tests also end up duplicating setup/teardown logic and are brittle.

We need a multi-process replication rig that:
- Boots N real `bd` daemons in separate repos/data/runtime/config roots under `./tmp`.
- Wires real replication connections (TCP) with optional tailnet-style fault injection.
- Provides helpers to apply ops, poll state, and assert convergence on watermarks/heads.
- Cleans up all daemons and proxy processes reliably.

**Files:** new fixture module (tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs), updated integration tests under tests/integration/daemon/.
design:
**Design**
- Create `tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs` with:
  - `ReplRig::new(node_count, options)` -> builds per-node dirs in `./tmp`, sets `BD_STORE_ID`, `BD_DATA_DIR`, `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`, `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, `BD_NO_AUTO_UPGRADE`.
  - `Node` helper with `bd_cmd()`, `init()`, `start_daemon()`, `shutdown()`; stores `repo_dir`, `runtime_dir`, `data_dir`, `config_dir`, `listen_addr`, `replica_id`.
  - `bootstrap_replica()` that runs `bd init`, reads `meta.json` for replica_id (StoreMeta), then shuts down daemon before configuring peers.
  - `write_replication_config()` to emit `beads.toml` with listen addr + peers + allowed namespaces.
- Fault injection:
  - Allow `ReplRig` to optionally insert a `TailnetProxy` per link (reusing `tailnet_proxy` binary) with a profile (tailnet/none or custom). Expose `options.fault_profile` and `options.seed`.
- Provide helpers:
  - `create_issue(node, title)` returning id
  - `wait_for_show(node, id, timeout)`
  - `admin_status(node)` returning JSON payload
  - `assert_converged(namespaces)` using admin watermarks/heads across all nodes.
- Add at least one slow integration test (e.g. 3-node tailnet profile) using the rig instead of custom wiring.

**Files:** tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs, tests/integration/daemon/* (new test), tests/integration/fixtures/mod.rs
title: Multi-process replication rig helper (real daemons + fault injection)
